mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 215 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as bond yields rise and equity valuations contract.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and employment (PAYEMS) signals robust supply-side expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: The joint growth of INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 70% of cases, with a Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% indicates tight labor markets, pushing wage growth and corporate margins higher, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of periods when UNRATE is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) above 950 thousand units signals strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through increased construction and related sector activity.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST exceeds 950.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDP (GDPC1) below 1.5% growth signals weak supply-side performance, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as recession risks materialize.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods when GDPC1 growth is below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong aggregate demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sustained corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: The joint growth of RSAFS and GDPC1 fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 70% of cases, with a Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 2.5% signals a tightening cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as borrowing costs rise and equity valuations contract.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%.
